metadata.scheme_name: Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th

raw_data.details: The scheme "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to support students from economically weaker backgrounds by reducing the burden of education-related expenses and promoting school attendance. The scheme ensures that eligible students from Classes 1st to 8th studying in government and aided schools receive ₹900 for three pairs of school uniforms annually, enhancing their dignity and inclusion in the school environment.
raw_data.benefits: Financial Assistance: ₹900 is provided to each eligible student for the purchase of three pairs of school uniforms.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be a student. The applicant should belong to the state of Gujarat. The applicant should be studying in Class 1st to Class 8th. The applicant should be enrolled in a government or government-aided primary school. The applicant should belong to the Economically Weaker Sections.
raw_data.application_process:
OnlineHow to Register:
Step 1: Visit the [Digital Gujarat](https://www.digitalgujarat.gov.in/) portal.
Step 2: The user should click on the "Click to register" option. Fill all details and click on "Save". How to Log In:
Step 1: Visit the [Digital Gujarat](https://www.digitalgujarat.gov.in/) portal.
Step 2: The user should click on the "Login" option.
Step 3: Enter all details and click on "Login". How to Apply for a New Service:
Step 1: After logging in, select the relevant department.
Step 2: Choose the specific service to apply for within that department.
Step 3: Fill out all required details in the application form.
Step 4: Upload, attach, or browse necessary documents. Alternatively, import documents directly from DigiLocker.
Step 5: Carefully review all entered information.
Step 6: Submit the application.
Step 7: Once the application is completed, proceed to payment to download the certificate.
Step 8: Upon successful payment, the certificate will be available for download. Digital Gujarat (Help Desk):Contact No : 18002335500
raw_data.documents_required: Identity Proof. Residence Proof. Passport-size Photograph. Academic Certificates. Bank Details. Category Proof (EWS). Any Other documents as required.

Any Other documents as required. Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the main objective of the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme? The primary objective of the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme is to support economically backward students by reducing the financial burden of education through the provision of funds specifically meant for the purchase of school uniforms, thereby encouraging regular school attendance and promoting educational inclusion. Who is eligible to receive assistance under the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme? Students who are studying in Classes 1 to 8 in government or government-aided primary schools and belong to the economically weaker sections are eligible to receive financial assistance under the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme launched by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat. How much financial support is provided under the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme? Under the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme, eligible students are provided with a one-time financial assistance of ₹900 annually, which is intended to cover the cost of three pairs of school uniforms for the academic year. Can students studying in private schools apply for the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme? Students who are enrolled in private unaided schools are not eligible to apply for the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme, as it is strictly intended for those studying in government and government-aided primary schools in the state of Gujarat. Does the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme apply to students of all income groups? The "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme is exclusively meant for students from economically weaker sections, and although no specific income threshold is mentioned, applicants must be recognized as financially disadvantaged according to the guidelines set by the implementing authority. How can an applicant apply for this scheme? Applicants can apply through the [Digital Gujarat](https://www.digitalgujarat.gov.in/) portal. What happens if incorrect or incomplete documents are submitted along with the application? If a student submits incorrect or incomplete documents during the application process, the application may be rejected or delayed until proper verification is completed, and such discrepancies can result in the forfeiture of scholarship benefits for that year. Is there any relaxation or reservation criteria in the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme? There is no explicit mention of reservation, relaxation, or preference under the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme; all eligible economically backward students studying in Classes 1 to 8 in recognized schools are considered equally for benefits. Sources And References[Guidelines](https://mariyojana.gujarat.gov.in/Schemeatoz.aspx)

faqs.question: What is the main objective of the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme?
faqs.answer: The primary objective of the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme is to support economically backward students by reducing the financial burden of education through the provision of funds specifically meant for the purchase of school uniforms, thereby encouraging regular school attendance and promoting educational inclusion.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to receive assistance under the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme?
faqs.answer: Students who are studying in Classes 1 to 8 in government or government-aided primary schools and belong to the economically weaker sections are eligible to receive financial assistance under the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme launched by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat.

faqs.question: How much financial support is provided under the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme?
faqs.answer: Under the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme, eligible students are provided with a one-time financial assistance of ₹900 annually, which is intended to cover the cost of three pairs of school uniforms for the academic year.

faqs.question: Can students studying in private schools apply for the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme?
faqs.answer: Students who are enrolled in private unaided schools are not eligible to apply for the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme, as it is strictly intended for those studying in government and government-aided primary schools in the state of Gujarat.

faqs.question: Does the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme apply to students of all income groups?
faqs.answer: The "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme is exclusively meant for students from economically weaker sections, and although no specific income threshold is mentioned, applicants must be recognized as financially disadvantaged according to the guidelines set by the implementing authority.

faqs.question: How can an applicant apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants can apply through the [Digital Gujarat](https://www.digitalgujarat.gov.in/) portal.

faqs.question: What happens if incorrect or incomplete documents are submitted along with the application?
faqs.answer: If a student submits incorrect or incomplete documents during the application process, the application may be rejected or delayed until proper verification is completed, and such discrepancies can result in the forfeiture of scholarship benefits for that year.

faqs.question: Is there any relaxation or reservation criteria in the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme?
faqs.answer: There is no explicit mention of reservation, relaxation, or preference under the "Uniform Assistance for Economically Backward Class Students Studying in Class 1st to 8th" scheme; all eligible economically backward students studying in Classes 1 to 8 in recognized schools are considered equally for benefits.
